div1.entryFree:
Mutually, 1) on both sides: your most offenceful act was m. committed, Meas. II, 3, 27.
2) in return: who m. hath answered my affection, Wiv. IV, 6, 10.
3) in common, all together: pinch him, fairies, m. Wiv. V, 5, 103. the other instruments did see and hear, devise, instruct, walk, feel, and, m. participate, did minister unto the appetite, Cor. I, 1, 106.
